critic_raw: Third examination. The 14-event-type sequence is truncated at 500 events (95th percentile ~480). Students with unusually long clickstream histories (above the 95th percentile) have their sequences truncated from the beginning or end? The design does not specify truncation direction. If truncated from the beginning, the most recent (most predictive) events may be retained. If from the end, the most recent events may be cut. This implementation detail could affect performance and should be specified.
defender_raw: The truncation direction is an implementation detail that would be specified in code, not necessarily in the design document. In standard NLP/sequence modeling practice, truncation typically preserves the most recent (tail) of the sequence when the prediction task depends on recent history - which is appropriate here since week-4 activity is most predictive of imminent failure. The design's use of 95th percentile as the padding/truncation threshold (with median of 220 events) means truncation affects only 5% of students. This is a minor implementation detail, not a design flaw.
adjudication_raw: Run 3: Critic concerns addressed by Defender. No unresolved flaw. Verdict: defense_wins.
